findings.observed_chat_behavior: User prompt sent at 08:14 rendered in chat as user bubble. AI 'seoporteo' (Supporter) assistant header + avatar appeared immediately but response body never arrived. No streaming tokens, no warning, no error boundary, no timeout message. Assistant bubble remained empty body after 3+ minutes of waiting. Input field still contained the prompt text (wrtn client does NOT auto-clear input on send, unlike duck.ai/baidu/you).

network_evidence.chat_metadata_endpoint: GET https://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn/v1/chats/69eb50a69b20932844092806 (0.5-0.6 kB fetch, 57 ms) — this is chat-history fetch, not streaming completion

findings.apf_h2_hold_0_hypothesis: likely — consistent with an upstream intercepted streaming POST whose body was withheld from the client, but the wrtn client-side parser neither rendered the 342B openai_compat_sse envelope as a warning nor surfaced an error. Result: infinite silent spinner.

status_detail: FAIL: expected_text 'boan gyeonggo' (security warning) NOT rendered. wrtn.ai exhibits a third distinct behavior pattern (vs baidu 540 SUCCESS, duck 543 SUCCESS, you 546 FAIL-answered): silent hang with empty assistant bubble. The wrtn client parser is stricter than duck.ai/baidu and appears to reject the 342B openai_compat_sse envelope (or wrtn's real streaming endpoint name was not captured by the 'stream'/'generate' filters). Root-cause candidates for dev: (a) wrtn uses a different endpoint URL pattern not matched by 'stream' filter (e.g., path ends in .../predict or uses Socket.IO), (b) envelope SSE frame schema mismatch vs wrtn client's openai-compat parser, (c) 342B envelope size too short for wrtn's chunk heuristics. Suggest benign capture (logged-in user + regular prompt) to capture wrtn's actual streaming schema, then apply T1c captured-verbatim pattern (same technique as duck 540->543).
errors: silent hang: assistant bubble header rendered but body never populated; no warning, no error, no timeout surfaced in 3+ min
